name: kanban-board
description: Port of the manual builder trial: a kanban board built from scratch, with card identity that must survive moving between columns.
prompt:
This workspace is a freshly scaffolded Native SDK native-frontend app (see README.md; the view is src/app.native, the logic src/main.zig). Replace the counter with a small kanban board app.

Requirements:
- Three columns named exactly "Todo", "Doing", and "Done", laid out side by side, each showing its column title and the cards currently in it. Seed the board with at least five cards spread across the columns.
- Each card shows its title and has controls to move it to the previous/next column (a card in Todo can only move right, a card in Done only left).
- A card is the same logical item wherever it lives: when a card moves between columns its identity must be preserved, not recreated — the automation snapshot's widget id for that card should stay stable across the move.
- Show a status bar with the count of cards per column, updating live.
- Keep all logic in src/main.zig (Model, Msg, update); the markup only binds values and dispatches messages.
- Validate the markup with `native markup check src/app.native`, and keep `zig build test` green — extend src/tests.zig so the tests move a card between columns through the real dispatch path and assert the counts change.

When you are done, make sure `zig build test` passes and summarize what you built.

checks.description: cards use global-key so identity survives reparenting
live snapshot shows the three columns and card move buttons

criteria: Model/Msg/update follow The Elm Architecture idiomatically: card movement is expressed as clear messages, edge rules (Todo can't move left, Done can't move right) live in update or the view's conditionals sensibly, no redundant state.
Card identity is handled correctly: global-key usage is principled (stable per logical card), and the column/card data model makes movement cheap and clear.
Tests are meaningful: they move cards through the real dispatch path and assert both the source and destination columns, including the edge rules.
The markup is clean: columns are factored without copy-paste (template or well-structured loops), bindings and messages only.
checks.description: quality of the kanban app beyond compliance
